Case Study UX · Fintech · Automotive August 2022

TATA Motors
Financing Platform

Digitising the end-to-end commercial vehicle financing journey for Tata Motors — shifting a fully offline, paper-heavy process to a transparent, real-time digital platform for customers, DSEs, and financiers.

Role
UX Designer
Yuj Designs
Client
Tata Motors Ltd
$44B organisation
Platform
Mobile App
Web Platform
Year
2022
Yuj Designs
$44B
Client — India's largest automotive OEM
0
Paperwork — fully digital financing journey
3
Steps to get a vehicle — Verify, Field, Credit
Real-time
Loan approvals across multiple financiers
01 — About the Client

Tata Motors — India's leading automotive OEM

Tata Motors Limited is a $44 Billion organisation and the leading global automobile manufacturing company. Its diverse portfolio spans cars, SUVs, trucks, buses, and defence vehicles — and is one of India's largest OEMs offering smart e-mobility solutions.

02 — Vision & Goals

Building transparency across the entire value chain

The platform aimed to deliver a seamless, end-to-end digital buying journey for commercial vehicle customers and DSEs (Dealer Sales Executives) — eliminating paper, reducing turnaround time, and bringing multiple financiers onto a single platform.

Business Vision
Transparency, efficiency, convenience
End-to-end digital buying journey. Straight-through process for customers and DSEs to onboard and scale up quickly.
Platform Vision
Reduce TAT across the value chain
Allow multiple financiers on the platform to provide the best offer. Real-time approvals to close sales faster.
03 — Research

In-person interviews conducted across India

Research was conducted pan-India across DSEs, financiers, and CV customers. We found strong similarities in digital dependency — phone calls, WhatsApp, and email were the primary tools for coordinating day-to-day activities.

04 — User Personas

Two key users — the DSE and the Financier

Persona 01 — DSE (Dealer Sales Executive)
Pain Points
Admin overload kills productivity
Takes time to process documents. Difficult viewing client data. Creating reports manually. Loses track of leads.
Goals
Create leads quickly
Check customer eligibility for finance. Help tech-savvy customers. Support rejected loan customers.
Key Insight
"If I keep doing admin jobs, my productivity might decrease."
DSE load must reduce digitally. Offerings like 'Samridhi' should be fully digital.
Persona 02 — Financier
Pain Points
System and document gaps
Bank systems unreliable. Dealers don't commit to vehicle. Dealership delays take 15–20 days for approval.
Motivations
Relationship-driven
Customer relationships, online paperwork, easy communication with dealership, and customer satisfaction.
Key Insight
"Today customers are very smart, we cannot fool them."
Post-approval, customers should submit documents directly in the portal — no back and forth.
05 — Design Focus

Conceptual focus areas from affinity mapping

Conversion
Increase data & conversion rate
Reduce drop-off and bounce rate. Increase online sessions and onboarding. Grow unique visitors and revisits.
Usability
Error prevention & assistance
Increase help and assistance touchpoints. Ensure product/service understanding. Reduce drop-offs across the funnel.
06 — User Flow

Overall user flow — from discovery to approval

Two parallel journeys designed: in-store and digital. A chatbot provides help throughout, and a multi-financier comparison step ensures customers always get the best offer.

07 — Concept Design

From wireframes to visual design

Wireframing
Detail Design
Visual Design
08 — Hi-Fi Design

Components & next steps

A complete component library was built alongside the hi-fi screens — covering dropdowns, eligibility tags (CIBIL compatibility indicators), and a 3-step next steps module to guide customers post-approval.

Component 01
Smart dropdowns
Consistent dropdown behaviour across vehicle search, financier selection, and form fields.
Component 02
Compatibility tags
CIBIL-based compatibility labels (100% / 50% / 25%) and "Your current financier" tag surfaced contextually.
Component 03
3-step next steps
Post-approval: Verify → Field Investigation → Credit Transfer — giving customers full visibility into what happens next.
